steps.title: Use the one-half power
steps.content:
An exponent of $\frac{1}{2}$ means square root. The exponent applies to the entire base $63y$.
$$
(63y)^{\frac{1}{2}}=\sqrt{63y}
$$

steps.title: Match the equivalent form
steps.content: The whole product stays under the radical because the whole product is raised to the $\frac{1}{2}$ power. The matching expression is $$ \sqrt{63y} $$ The answer is C.
